author: @musikSkool
text:
I heard a Comedian once say that before he gets into a debate on Politics he asks what they would need to see to change their mind, and if they say that nothing will change their mind, then they are too biased to discuss anything with.  If people aren't willing to do an experiment to see if there is something they don't know then science will be held back.

  I can't tell you how many times I've seen a flat earther willing to do an experiment, and it proved them wrong, but the fact they were willing to do the experiment means they are at least open-minded to do doing real scientific experimentation to find the facts, instead of claim they are definitely true and it doesn't need to be proven.

author: @MountainCatBob
text:
Thank you for the cautionary note! It is still not a safe to undermine the old guard materialists.

Scientific paradigms are changing. The old paradigm no longer serves us because according to the Standard Model, more than 90% of the entire universe is "Dark Matter" and "Dark Energy" or their math won't work out right. In addition, the Standard Model can't even explain why gravity works or the many anomalies that plague this old materialistic model, and the Electric Universe explains all anomalies by using more accurate application of math, such as plasma equations. But we have to remember that many very smart people have spent their lives and reputations on believing in the Standard Model and will certainly NOT go quietly into the night! Their livelihoods depend on being right!

Since Occam's Razor explains that the simplest explanation is best, and since all the anomalies are explained away by a physicist named Nassim Haramein, who published his new Unified Field Theory (find papers here: https://www.spacefed.com/research), and his explanations unite all fields of physics without using fudge factors,  the Electric Universe is backed by the latest UFT and is certainly more comprehensible to everyone. Also, the math works out for all scales.

An interesting side note is the difference between people living the new Spiritual Paradigm, where we all share ideas and information, vs. the stealing of ideas, patents, maximal profits, and cut-throat competition of the old Materialistic Paradigms.

author: @calvincheney7405
text:
Robert, I began my journey into EU theory on a  circuitous route. I'm from Michigan & we owned gravel pits as I grew up. I studied TV & radio production & service, joined the military &  became a Combat Engineer & then an Intelligence Analyst. Eventually, I married a Filipina. So, glacial action ~ electromagnetic spectrum ~ soils & terrain analysis, satellite optical & listening platforms ~ interest in earthquake & volcano tracking & forecasting ~ Immanuel Velikovsky, a catastrophist that wrote Worlds in Collision ~ chaos geology theory ~ solar forcing of weather, tectonic & volcanic activity ~ long cycle patterns in galactic procession through the universe ~ periodic nova, ice ages, pole flips, worldwide & regional tsunami, subduction zone plate slips on the semi-viscous region at the mantel boundary. 
Thus, my dear gifted Robert, not only is science manipulated for the reasons you've so eloquently outlined. It is also a concerted effort to maintain stability in society. So that folks blindly stumble along in this consumerist drunken stupor making long term investments & paying their bills on time. Rather, a conscientious public living sustainably, recognizing a grand solar disaster is capable of instantly driving the world back to the stone age with an X 100+ solar flare which could easily occur in our lifetime is the last thing those who control our perception of scientific fact want us to contemplate. Love, Calvin~

author: @Rhannmah
text:
There's many, MANY problems with the electric universe ideas, but the most important one is that it makes no predictions that the current theories in cosmology don't already predict, and in a simpler fashion.

A new would be theory has to explain everything we already know and understand, and then explain some other phenomena that current theories don't explain.

author: @Kangsteri
text: The issue with science is math and space :D Many people have real phobia against those. That's why I always try to compare subjects with simple action in nature. It's much more intuitive to understand. For example magnetism/gravity is more easy to see if I say that you have the relatively same amount of gravity in you as the planet. But the Earth's mass is so much bigger that you can't pull it by jumping to air. Gravity and magnetism is the same force that keeps water droplets hanging from the roof, or your finger ;) Electricity is in the force of surface tension. That means the more there is potential electricity, the less it has surface tension, cause the energy wants to separate from it to be in neutral state. That is not intuitive, cause we often confuse the density of mass to potential energy/gravity.

author: @gshingles
text: Big topic Rob. I've only recently become aware of the ramifications of essentially enforcing a prescribed scientific doctrine. It is one of the cornerstones of Eric Lerner's arguments for what is holding back the development of fusion energy. I don't agree or disagree with him--I have nowhere near the knowledge to do either, but his hypothesis is that the Universe is more electric in nature and that plasma is the key to the creation of matter, galaxy formation, etc. He is researching focused plasma fusion and I'm glad he is doing it, the rewards would be immense if proven out. The backlash he gets from the scientific community has been a real eye opener though. Challenge one tiny aspect of current Scientific thinking, eg the Big Bang you are essentially committing scientific heresy and will not be welcome in the tent. :) My attitude is, what if he is right?

author: @TheBaconWizard
text:
There's a whole lot to be said around this subject, and I don't want to post a wall of text. 
So to summarise, I think education that uses ordinary language about what science actually IS, HOW it works, and a frank discussion of what science is NOT and some of the ways it doesn't really work too well, all with a wider context, are entirely missing. 
Churchill said of democracy "It is the worst from of government, except for all the other ones we've tried". Science is the worst way to understand the natural universe, except for the other systems we've tried so far. 
We should know this before anyone tries to teach any science at school imo, as it is fundamental to all future discussion on the topic and far more important for most people than knowing how to do a litmus test or what colour sodium burns (although making stinks and bangs or a working motor from a paperclip, is great education too!)
It can can inform many other decision-making processes in life that aren't scientific in nature. Justice and politics for example. In the age of  global communications it worries me that the majority of our species has access to all information (ish) but it is like finding fish in a vast sea of bullshit, and the majority don't know how to do research or have good critical thinking skills.

author: @CCoburn3
text:
We know cosmological physics hasn't made any significant advances since they came up with string theory. And in order to get their equations to work, they have to invent "dark matter" and "dark energy" -- neither of which can be detected outside of their formulas. (Sounds a lot like the "luminiferous ether" that was supposed to be the medium for transmitting light.) But they treat everyone who suggests another theory is treated like a charlatan. As you point out, THAT is not "science."

PS: I'd put James Clerk Maxwell on the list -- bumping off any of the other three you want.

replies.author: @TheBaconWizard
replies.text:
I'd dispute that characterisation. they didn't "invent" dark matter, or dark energy. They are simply placeholder names for a phenomenon that they can see happening. SOMETHING is exerting a gravitational force, but it cannot be seen in the electromagnetic spectrum thus-far. It therefore gets an assumption of being "matter" while acknowledging it's an assumption, and is called "dark" because its' unseen. That's ALL anyone has said about it. A similar deal with energy. 
String theory is, so far, completely unfalsifiable so it has gone nowhere and that will remain the case until either more work on it comes-up with something we can actually test, or technology advances enough to test things currently beyond our ken. 
The confirmation of the higgs field by actually observing a higgs-boson at CERN (and within the lifetime of the chap who's work predicted it too, which is nice!) is imo a huge advance.

replies.author: @CCoburn3
replies.text:
@TheBaconWizard We are told that the entire earth can pass through a sea of "dark matter" and never detect it. Before Einstein, they said the same thing about luminiferous ether. When "scientists" can ONLY explain their theories by referring to undetectable "matter" or "energy," that's a good sign they are wrong. 

It's not that I object to them being wrong. In fact, whether they are right or wrong has absolutely NO bearing on anything that will EVER happen in the world. But their attitude is unscientific. When they create a theory that cannot possibly be tested, that's a good indication that they should think again. But they REFUSE to think again. And they refuse to be open to other ideas.
